title: Tacsonia pennipes

It has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and gastrointestinal disorders, due to its anti-inflammatory, antipyretic, and antimicrobial properties, which contribute to its potential health benefits. The bioactive constituents of Tacsonia pennipes include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
